title: hiraea fimbriata
intro: Hiraea fimbriata, also known as the Fimbriated Hiraea, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of New Caledonia. It is a member of the family Malpighiaceae, and its name is derived from the Latin words 'hiraea' meaning 'tall shrub' and 'fimbriata' meaning 'fringed'. The medicinal properties of Hiraea fimbriata include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The herbal preparations of the plant include extracts and infusions, which can be used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, providing several health benefits to those who use it.
